title: Cnidoscolus froesii
taxonomy: Cnidoscolus froesii is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae and belongs to the order Malpighiales. This plant is native to South America and its classification is based on its characteristic morphology and genetic features. At the genus level, Cnidoscolus froesii is grouped with other species that share similar characteristics, such as the presence of milky latex and the shape of their leaves. The classification of Cnidoscolus froesii is also influenced by its phylogenetic relationships with other plants in the same family. By understanding its classification, researchers can better comprehend the evolutionary history and diversity of this plant species.

side_effects: Cnidoscolus froesii, a plant native to the tropical regions of South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. However, its use has also been associated with side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. The sap of the plant, which contains urushiol, a compound similar to that found in poison ivy, can cause severe skin reactions in some individuals. Furthermore, long-term consumption of Cnidoscolus froesii may lead to an imbalance of electrolytes and other health problems. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of using this plant as a medicinal agent.
